briefdescription.para: This function enables fast link training in the core controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
width val to be used over the main link based on one of the following selects:

briefdescription.para: This function disables fast link training in the core controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the max lane count supported from DPCD.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the max link rate supported from Rx DPCD register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads MST_CAP from Rx DPCD register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates link training sequence with the Rx.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function configures DPCD Rx Field.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function configures CCTL enhance framing enable bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function configures CCTL enhance framing enable bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Training Aux Read Interval register of sink DPCD.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the training aux interval.

detaileddescription.para: The lowest time is 400us. All other WaitTimes are direct multiples of lowest time and value read from DPCD register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will do a burst AUX read from the RX device over the AUX channel.

detaileddescription.para: The contents of the status registers will be stored for later use by XDp_TxCheckClockRecovery, XDp_TxCheckChannelEqualization, and XDp_TxAdjVswingPreemp.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the AUX read was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates that the clock recovery sequence during link training was successful - the RX device's link clock and data recovery unit has realized and maintained the frequency lock for all lanes currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMmiDp instance.
is the number of lanes to check.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device's clock recovery PLL has achieved frequency lock for all lanes in use.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the adjusted voltage swing and pre-emphasis level settings from the Rx DPCD register and modify the drive settings accordingly during link training.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing the settings was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function sets current voltage swing and pre-emphasis level settings from the LinkConfig structure to Phy and DPCD.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is a pointer to the array used for preparing a burst write over the AUX channel.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if writing the settings was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the training pattern to be used during link training for both the DisplayPort TX core and the RX device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
selects the pattern to be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the pattern was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Rx Dpcd link rate as well as Phy link rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
value to program.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Rx Dpcd lane count as well as Phy lane count.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
value to program.

briefdescription.para: This function runs the clock recovery sequence as part of link training.

detaileddescription.para:
The sequence is as follows: 0) Start signaling at the minimum voltage swing, pre-emphasis, and post- cursor levels. 1) Transmit training pattern 1 over the main link with symbol scrambling disabled. 2) The clock recovery loop. If clock recovery is unsuccessful after MaxIterations loop iterations, return. 2a) Wait for at least the period of time specified in the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) register, TRAINING_AUX_RD_INTERVAL. 2b) Check if all lanes have achieved clock recovery lock. If so, return. 2c) Check if the same voltage swing level has been used 5 consecutive times or if the maximum level has been reached. If so, return. 2d) Adjust the voltage swing, pre-emphasis, and post-cursor levels as requested by the RX device. 2e) Loop back to 2a.
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XDp instance.
The next training state:
XMMIDP__TS_CHANNEL_EQUALIZATION if the clock recovery sequence completed successfully.
XMMIDP__TS_FAILURE if writing the drive settings to the RX device was unsuccessful.
XMMIDP__TS_ADJUST_LINK_RATE if the clock recovery sequence did not complete successfully.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function is reached if either the clock recovery or the channel equalization process failed during training.

detaileddescription.para: As a result, the data rate will be downshifted, and training will be re-attempted (starting with clock recovery) at the reduced data rate. If the data rate is already at 1.62 Gbps, a downshift in lane count will be attempted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XMMIDP_TS_ADJUST_LANE_COUNT if the minimal data rate is already in use. Re-attempt training at a reduced lane count.
XMMIDP_TS_CLOCK_RECOVERY otherwise. Re-attempt training.

briefdescription.para: This function is reached if either the clock recovery or the channel equalization process failed during training, and a minimal data rate of 1.62 Gbps was being used.

detaileddescription.para: As a result, the number of lanes in use will be reduced, and training will be re-attempted (starting with clock recovery) at this lower lane count.

simplesect.para: Training will be re-attempted with the maximum data rate being used with the reduced lane count to train at the main link at the maximum bandwidth possible.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XMMIDP_TS_FAILURE if only one lane is already in use.
XMMIDP_TS_CLOCK_RECOVERY otherwise. Re-attempt training.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Rx DPCD MaxDownspread register bits.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Rx DPCD revision number.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Rx DPCD capability registers to initiate link training.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the training process succeeded.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates that the channel equalization sequence during link training was successful - the RX device has achieved channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpPsu instance.
is the number of lanes to check.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device has achieved channel equalization symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes in use.

briefdescription.para: This function runs the channel equalization sequence as part of link training.

detaileddescription.para: The sequence is as follows: 0) Start signaling with the same drive settings used at the end of the clock recovery sequence. 1) Transmit training pattern 2 (or 3) over the main link with symbol scrambling disabled. 2) The channel equalization loop. If channel equalization is unsuccessful after 5 loop iterations, return. 2a) Wait for at least the period of time specified in the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) register, TRAINING_AUX_RD_INTERVAL. 2b) Check if all lanes have achieved channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment. If so, return. 2c) Check if the same voltage swing level has been used 5 consecutive times or if the maximum level has been reached. If so, return. 2d) Adjust the voltage swing, pre-emphasis, and post-cursor levels as requested by the RX device. 2e) Loop back to 2a. For a more detailed description of the channel equalization sequence, see section 3.5.1.2.2 of the DisplayPort 1.4a specification document.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para:
The next training state:
XDP_TX_TS_SUCCESS if training succeeded.
XDP_TX_TS_FAILURE if writing the drive settings to the RX device was unsuccessful.
XDP_TX_TS_ADJUST_LINK_RATE if, after 5 loop iterations, the channel equalization sequence did not complete successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the Rx DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates the receiver has achieved and maintained clock recovery, channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes currently in use.The DP 1.4 spec specifies checking for the above bits max 5 times before retraining link at lower link rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XMmiDp instance.
is the number of lanes to check.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device has maintained clock recovery, channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.

briefdescription.para: This function runs the link training process.

detaileddescription.para: It is implemented as a state machine, with each state returning the next state. First, the clock recovery sequence will be run; if successful, the channel equalization sequence will run. If either the clock recovery or channel equalization sequence failed, the link rate or the number of lanes used will be reduced and training will be re-attempted. If training fails at the minimal data rate, 1.62 Gbps with a single lane, training will no longer re-attempt and fail.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the training process succeeded.
